Implements a panic window application for Pebble firmware, displaying error codes and handling resets.

# Purpose
The code defines a panic handling application for a Pebble device. It is responsible for displaying a panic screen when a critical error occurs. The application uses various libraries for graphics, fonts, and system management to render a bitmap image and display an error code on the device screen. The `PanicWindowAppData` structure holds the window and layer data necessary for rendering the panic screen. The function [`prv_update_proc`](<#prv_update_proc>) is responsible for drawing the bitmap and error code text on the screen.

The application also includes functionality to handle button clicks, which triggers a system reset through the [`prv_panic_reset_callback`](<#prv_panic_reset_callback>) function. The [`prv_handle_init`](<#prv_handle_init>) function initializes the panic window, sets up the click configuration, and pushes the window onto the application stack. The [`s_main`](<#s_main>) function serves as the entry point for the application, initializing the panic handling and starting the event loop. The [`panic_app_get_app_info`](<#panic_app_get_app_info>) function provides metadata about the application, including its UUID and run level, indicating that it operates at a critical run level.

# Global Variables

---
### sad\_watch
- **Type**: ``uint8_t[]``
- **Description**: An array of unsigned 8-bit integers that contains binary data. This data is used to initialize a bitmap image in the application.
- **Use**: Used to initialize a `GBitmap` object for rendering a bitmap image on the screen.


# Data Structures

---
### PanicWindowAppData
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - ``window``: A `Window` object that represents the main window of the application.
    - ``layer``: A `Layer` object that represents a drawable layer within the window.
- **Description**: Contains two members, `window` and `layer`, which are used to manage the graphical user interface components of a panic window application. The `window` member is responsible for the main application window, while the `layer` member is used for drawing and updating the visual content within that window.


# Functions

---
### prv\_update\_proc<!-- {{#callable:prv_update_proc}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/core_apps/panic_window_app.c#L53>)

Renders a bitmap and an error code text on a given layer using a graphics context.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``layer``: A pointer to the `Layer` object where the bitmap and text will be drawn.
    - ``ctx``: A pointer to the `GContext` object used for drawing operations.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Sets the compositing mode of the graphics context to `GCompOpAssignInverted` to invert the colors of the drawn graphics.
    - Initializes a `GBitmap` object with the `sad_watch` bitmap data.
    - Defines a [`GRect`](<../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GRect>) structure `bitmap_dest_rect` to specify the destination rectangle for the bitmap on the layer.
    - Draws the `sad_watch_bitmap` onto the layer at the position specified by `bitmap_dest_rect`.
    - Retrieves the system font `FONT_KEY_GOTHIC_14` and assigns it to `error_code_face`.
    - Defines a [`GRect`](<../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GRect>) structure `text_dest_rect` to specify the destination rectangle for the error code text on the layer.
    - Formats the current error code as a hexadecimal string and stores it in `text_buffer`.
    - Draws the formatted error code text onto the layer at the position specified by `text_dest_rect` using the `error_code_face` font.
- **Output**: No return value; the function performs drawing operations on the provided layer.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`gbitmap_init_with_data`](<../../applib/graphics/gbitmap.c.md#gbitmap_init_with_data>)
    - [`GRect`](<../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GRect>)


---
### prv\_panic\_reset\_callback<!-- {{#callable:prv_panic_reset_callback}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/core_apps/panic_window_app.c#L72>)

Initiates a system reset with a specific reboot reason when a panic occurs.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``data``: A pointer to data passed to the callback function, not used in this function.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Create a `RebootReason` structure with `code` set to `RebootReasonCode_LauncherPanic` and `extra` set to the current error code from `launcher_panic_get_current_error()`.
    - Call `reboot_reason_set()` with the `RebootReason` structure to set the reboot reason.
    - Call `system_reset()` to reset the system.
- **Output**: No return value; the function performs a system reset.


---
### prv\_panic\_button\_click\_handler<!-- {{#callable:prv_panic_button_click_handler}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/core_apps/panic_window_app.c#L82>)

Adds a callback function to handle a panic reset when a button is clicked.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``recognizer``: A reference to the click recognizer that identifies the button click event.
    - ``context``: A pointer to user-defined data or context, which is not used in this function.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls `launcher_task_add_callback` with `prv_panic_reset_callback` as the callback function and `NULL` as the data argument.
- **Output**: No output is returned as the function has a `void` return type.


---
### prv\_panic\_click\_config\_provider<!-- {{#callable:prv_panic_click_config_provider}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/core_apps/panic_window_app.c#L86>)

Configures single click event handlers for all buttons in a panic window context.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``context``: A pointer to the context data, which is not used in this function.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Iterates over all button IDs starting from `BUTTON_ID_BACK` to `NUM_BUTTONS`.
    - For each button ID, subscribes a single click event to the `prv_panic_button_click_handler` function using `window_single_click_subscribe`.
- **Output**: No return value; the function configures button click handlers.


---
### prv\_handle\_init<!-- {{#callable:prv_handle_init}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/core_apps/panic_window_app.c#L92>)

Initializes the panic window and its components for the application.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Allocates memory for `PanicWindowAppData` and assigns it to `data`.
    - Sets the user data in the application state to `data`.
    - Sets the application run level to `RunLevel_BareMinimum`.
    - Initializes the `window` within `data` with the name "Panic".
    - Configures the `window` to override the back button and sets its background color to black.
    - Sets the click configuration provider for the `window` to `prv_panic_click_config_provider`.
    - Checks if `CAPABILITY_HAS_HARDWARE_PANIC_SCREEN` is defined; if so, displays the panic screen with the current error.
    - If `CAPABILITY_HAS_HARDWARE_PANIC_SCREEN` is not defined, initializes the `layer` within `data`, sets its update procedure to `prv_update_proc`, and adds it as a child to the root layer of the `window`.
    - Pushes the `window` onto the application window stack without animation.
- **Output**: No output is returned as the function is of type `void`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`services_set_runlevel`](<../../services/services.c.md#services_set_runlevel>)
    - [`window_init`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_init>)
    - [`window_set_overrides_back_button`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_overrides_back_button>)
    - [`window_set_background_color`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_background_color>)
    - [`window_set_click_config_provider`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_click_config_provider>)

### s\_main<!-- {{#callable:s_main}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/core_apps/panic_window_app.c#L117>)

Initializes the panic window application and starts the event loop.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls `prv_handle_init()` to initialize the panic window application.
    - Calls `app_event_loop()` to start the application's event loop.
- **Output**: No output is returned as the function is of type `void`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_handle_init`](<#prv_handle_init>)


---
### panic\_app\_get\_app\_info<!-- {{#callable:panic_app_get_app_info}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/core_apps/panic_window_app.c#L122>)

Returns a pointer to a static `PebbleProcessMdSystem` structure containing metadata for the Panic App.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Defines a static `PebbleProcessMdSystem` structure `s_app_md` with metadata for the Panic App.
    - Sets the `main_func` to `s_main`, `visibility` to `ProcessVisibilityHidden`, and a specific UUID.
    - Sets the `name` to "Panic App" and `run_level` to `ProcessAppRunLevelCritical`.
    - Returns a pointer to the `s_app_md` structure cast to `const PebbleProcessMd*`.
- **Output**: A pointer to a `PebbleProcessMd` structure containing the Panic App's metadata.
